homepage.heroSubline: Legacy Electrical keeps North Texas homes safe and powered. Locally owned, trusted by families across DFW since 2012.
aboutText: Founded in 2012 by Dallas native James Holloway, Legacy Electrical began as a one-truck operation serving historic Lake Highlands homes. Today, we're proud to employ 20+ local electricians who understand the unique wiring needs of DFW's diverse architecture.

We believe electrical work should be predictable, safe, and hassle-free. Every project starts with a thorough assessment and ends with a system you can trust through Texas storms and heat waves alike.
From sponsoring Deep Ellum Arts Festival to volunteering wiring services for Oak Lawn community centers, we're committed to keeping Dallas neighborhoods thriving.

valueProps.title: Storm-Ready Wiring
valueProps.description: We fortify homes against North Texas' severe weather with surge protection and weatherproof installations.

homepage.ctaSubline: Call our local team at 555-ele-rq9 for prompt service across DFW.

services.name: Electrical Panel Upgrades
services.shortDescription: Modernize your home's power center for Dallas' extreme weather demands.
services.description:
Many Dallas homes still operate with 60-amp panels from the 1950s - dangerously outdated for today's appliances and North Texas storms. We replace these with 200-amp panels featuring whole-house surge protection, crucial for safeguarding electronics during our frequent lightning storms.

Our process begins with assessing your current panel's condition (common issues include Federal Pacific panels in older Plano homes). We handle all Dallas permits before installing Square D or Eaton panels with room for future expansions. For historic homes in McKinney, we discreetly route new conduit to preserve architectural integrity.

Upgraded panels prevent flickering lights during AC season and reduce fire risks in Arlington's aging ranch homes. They're also essential for adding EV chargers as more DFW residents switch to electric vehicles.
process: Step 1: Comprehensive load calculation accounting for Dallas heat pump demands
Step 2: Permit acquisition through your local municipality
Step 3: Safe removal of outdated equipment with minimal disruption
Step 4: Installation of weatherproof panel with whole-house surge protection
benefits: Prevents circuit overloads during Texas summer AC use
Meets current Dallas safety codes for home insurance
Accommodates future solar or EV charger installations
Reduces fire risks in older wiring systems

faq.question: How long does a panel upgrade take in Dallas homes?
faq.answer: Most Dallas-area upgrades complete in 6-8 hours. Historic homes may require extra time for careful conduit routing around original features.

faq.question: Will this prevent damage from Texas lightning storms?
faq.answer: While no system is 100% protective, our surge-equipped panels have saved Dallas clients thousands in appliance replacements after severe thunderstorms.

services.name: Whole-House Rewiring
services.shortDescription: Replace dangerous knob-and-tube wiring in Dallas' historic homes.
services.description:
The charming Tudors of Preston Hollow and Craftsman bungalows near Fort Worth's downtown often conceal hazardous knob-and-tube wiring. Our rewiring specialists preserve your home's character while bringing electricity up to modern Dallas safety standards.

We start by mapping every circuit, paying special attention to attics where Texas heat accelerates insulation breakdown. For plaster walls in historic districts, we use fishing techniques that minimize damage. All new wiring includes AFCI protection now required by Dallas building codes - critical for preventing electrical fires in drywall construction.

The process typically takes 3-5 days for a 2,000 sq ft Dallas home. We coordinate closely with homeowners to minimize disruption, often completing noisy work during school hours. Final inspection includes thermal imaging to identify any hotspots before drywall repairs begin.
process: Step 1: Detailed circuit mapping with voltage testing
Step 2: Strategic removal of outdated wiring with minimal wall impact
Step 3: Installation of moisture-resistant Romex wiring
Step 4: AFCI breaker installation and final city inspection
benefits: Eliminates fire risks from brittle old wiring
Allows safe use of modern appliances
Meets Dallas insurance requirements
Increases home value for resale

faq.question: How disruptive is rewiring my Dallas home?
faq.answer: We plan the work in phases to maintain power to essential areas. Most families stay home during the process with minimal inconvenience.

faq.question: Will this require drywall repairs?
faq.answer: Small access points are needed, but we partner with local drywall specialists who can make seamless repairs matching your home's texture.

services.name: Outdoor Lighting Installation
services.shortDescription: Illuminate your Dallas property with weather-resistant lighting solutions.
services.description:
DFW's long summer evenings and frequent outdoor entertaining demand durable landscape lighting. We design systems that withstand Texas' extreme conditions while enhancing your home's curb appeal.

For Frisco's newer constructions, we install low-voltage LED path lighting with automatic dusk-to-dawn operation. In Arlington's mature neighborhoods, we highlight architectural features with directional spotlights rated for hail protection. All wiring is buried 18" deep to prevent damage from Texas ground movement.

Our most popular package for Dallas homes includes motion-activated security lights near garages (a frequent target in urban areas), decorative wall sconces for porches, and pool area lighting rated for humid conditions. We use only brass or stainless steel fixtures that resist corrosion from our humid climate.
process: Step 1: Nighttime site evaluation to identify dark zones
Step 2: Custom lighting plan balancing security and ambiance
Step 3: Installation of underground conduit meeting Dallas codes
Step 4: Programming smart controls for energy efficiency
benefits: Deters crime in Dallas urban areas
Extends outdoor living through Texas summers
Withstands North Texas hail storms
Energy-efficient LED technology

faq.question: How do Dallas weather conditions affect lighting choices?
faq.answer: We avoid plastic components that become brittle in our heat. All connections are sealed against humidity, and fixtures have impact-resistant ratings for hail protection.

faq.question: Can lighting help with home security?
faq.answer: Properly placed lights reduce burglary risks by 50% according to Dallas PD statistics. We position fixtures to eliminate shadows where intruders might hide.

services.name: Generator Installation
services.shortDescription: Keep your Dallas home powered through Texas outages.
services.description:
After the 2021 Texas power crisis, many DFW homeowners invested in backup generators. We install permanent Generac systems sized perfectly for your home's needs during extended outages.

For Plano's larger homes, we typically recommend 22kW units capable of running multiple AC zones. McKinney's historic districts often require quieter inverter generators to meet noise ordinances. All installations include automatic transfer switches that activate within seconds of power loss - crucial for protecting food and medications during summer outages.

We handle the complex permitting process required by Dallas-area municipalities, including proper placement relative to windows and property lines. Maintenance contracts include twice-yearly testing before storm season and after winter freezes when generators see heaviest use.
process: Step 1: Load calculation accounting for Dallas cooling needs
Step 2: Site preparation meeting local setback requirements
Step 3: Professional installation with automatic transfer switch
Step 4: Comprehensive testing with your city's inspector
benefits: Maintains AC during Texas heat waves
Protects pipes during winter freezes
Automatic operation when traveling
Increases home value in DFW market

faq.question: How long can a generator power my Dallas home?
faq.answer: With proper fuel management, our installations typically run 5-7 days continuously - enough to weather most Texas grid emergencies.

faq.question: Will it be noisy in my neighborhood?
faq.answer: Modern generators operate at 60 decibels (quieter than a vacuum). We position them strategically to minimize sound impact on neighbors.

services.name: Ceiling Fan Installation
services.shortDescription: Improve comfort in Dallas homes with expert fan installations.
services.description:
Proper ceiling fan placement can reduce Dallas cooling costs by up to 40% during our brutal summers. We install high-efficiency fans with Texas-specific features like moisture-resistant motors and reversible airflow for seasonal use.

In Uptown condos with concrete ceilings, we use specialized mounting systems that prevent wobble. For Lake Highlands' vaulted ceilings, we install extended downrods for optimal air circulation. Every installation includes balancing to prevent the annoying humming common in Texas' humidity.

We particularly recommend fans with DC motors for Plano's larger bedrooms - they use 70% less energy than traditional models while moving more air. All our installations include remote controls or smart home integration for convenient operation.
process: Step 1: Assessment of ceiling structure and wiring
Step 2: Selection of appropriately sized fan for the room
Step 3: Secure mounting with vibration dampening
Step 4: Balancing and speed testing
benefits: Reduces AC dependency in Texas heat
Improves sleep comfort in humid conditions
Energy-efficient DC motor options
Quiet operation even at high speeds

faq.question: How many fans does my Dallas home need?
faq.answer: We recommend one fan per 200 sq ft in living areas and bedrooms. Great rooms may benefit from multiple units with synchronized controls.

faq.question: Can fans help in winter too?
faq.answer: Properly reversed fans circulate warm air trapped at ceilings, reducing heating costs during our mild Dallas winters.

services.name: EV Charger Installation
services.shortDescription: Power your electric vehicle with Dallas' fastest home charging.
services.description:
As Tesla's Austin gigafactory makes EVs more popular across Texas, DFW homeowners need reliable charging solutions. We install Level 2 chargers that fully power most vehicles overnight - perfect for Dallas commuters.

For Frisco's newer homes, we often install dual chargers in garages with smart load balancing. In older Dallas neighborhoods like Bishop Arts, we upgrade panels first to handle the additional demand. All our installations include GFCI protection required by Texas electrical code.

We position chargers conveniently near parking areas while maintaining proper clearance from flammable materials. Smart models allow scheduling charging during off-peak hours when Dallas electricity rates are lowest. Installation typically completes in one day with minimal disruption.
process: Step 1: Panel assessment for charging capacity
Step 2: Strategic placement planning for daily use
Step 3: Professional installation with weatherproof conduit
Step 4: Smart charger programming and testing
benefits: Fully charges overnight for Dallas commutes
Integrates with solar panel systems
Smart scheduling saves on electricity
Increases home value as EVs proliferate

faq.question: Will this overload my Dallas home's electrical system?
faq.answer: We perform detailed load calculations first. About 30% of older Dallas homes need panel upgrades before charger installation - we'll advise you honestly.

faq.question: How much faster than a standard outlet?
faq.answer: Level 2 chargers work 5-7x faster than trickle charging, adding about 25 miles of range per hour of charging.

services.name: Whole-House Surge Protection
services.shortDescription: Shield Dallas electronics from Texas storm damage.
services.description:
North Texas ranks #2 nationally for lightning strikes, making surge protection essential for DFW homes. We install professional-grade systems that protect your entire house, not just individual appliances.

Our most effective solution combines a main panel surge arrester with point-of-use protectors for sensitive equipment. This layered approach has saved Dallas clients thousands in damaged computers, smart home systems, and appliances after severe thunderstorms.

Installation takes about 2 hours for most Dallas homes. We recommend this service especially for Plano's tech-heavy households and Preston Hollow's historic homes with original wiring. All components are rated for Texas' voltage fluctuations and include lifetime warranties against failure.
process: Step 1: Assessment of your home's vulnerability points
Step 2: Main panel protector installation
Step 3: Strategic placement of secondary protectors
Step 4: System testing with simulated surges
benefits: Protects all home electronics simultaneously
Handles Texas-sized power fluctuations
Lifetime equipment warranties
Peace of mind during storm season

faq.question: How does this differ from power strips?
faq.answer: While power strips help, our whole-house systems stop surges at their entry point - before they can damage wiring or appliances not plugged into protectors.

faq.question: Is this necessary if I have a newer Dallas home?
faq.answer: Even new constructions benefit - modern electronics are more sensitive to small surges that may not trip breakers but gradually damage components.

reviews.name: Marcus T.
reviews.text: Legacy rewired our 1952 Lake Highlands ranch after finding dangerous knob-and-tube wiring behind the walls. They preserved all our original plaster while bringing everything up to code. Two years later, not a single flicker during Texas storms!

reviews.name: Sophia K.
reviews.text: When the 2023 ice storm hit, our Generac kept our Preston Hollow home warm while neighbors evacuated. The Legacy team installed it perfectly - passes city inspections with flying colors and runs so quietly we forget it's there until we need it.

reviews.name: Derrick W.
reviews.text: As Tesla owners in Frisco, we needed fast home charging. Legacy upgraded our panel and installed two Wall Connectors with perfect placement in our garage. The smart scheduling saves us about $80/month on electricity compared to supercharging.

reviews.name: Lisa M.
reviews.text: Our Uptown condo's concrete ceilings made fan installation tricky, but Legacy's special mounts worked perfectly. The DC motor fan is whisper-quiet and keeps our bedroom comfortable without cranking the AC all summer.

reviews.name: Carlos R.
reviews.text: After lightning fried our home theater system last spring, Legacy installed whole-house surge protection. When another storm hit this June, our system handled multiple direct strikes with zero damage. Worth every penny for Dallas homeowners.

reviews.name: Natalie B.
reviews.text: Legacy transformed our dark Bishop Arts backyard into an evening oasis. The path lighting withstands Texas storms, and the motion lights give peace of mind. They even matched the sconces to our historic home's style.

service-areas.title: Dallas Electrical Service Areas | Legacy
service-areas.description: Serving all DFW: Dallas, Fort Worth, Plano, Frisco, McKinney and Arlington electrical needs with local electricians since 2012.

serviceAreas.content:
Legacy Electrical proudly serves Fort Worth, TX, a vibrant city with a rich architectural heritage, including charming Craftsman bungalows near downtown. Our team is experienced in handling the electrical needs of older homes, ensuring they meet modern safety standards while preserving their historic charm. With Fort Worth's hot, humid summers, we also specialize in upgrading electrical systems to support efficient air conditioning and prevent overloads.

Whether you need panel upgrades, lighting installations, or emergency repairs, Legacy Electrical provides reliable service tailored to Fort Worth's unique homes. We understand the challenges of older wiring and offer solutions that blend seamlessly with your home's character while enhancing functionality.
serviceAreas.localContext: Fort Worth features historic Craftsman bungalows and a hot, humid climate that demands robust electrical systems.

serviceAreas.content:
Legacy Electrical is your trusted electrician in Plano, TX, serving the city's well-established subdivisions built primarily in the 1980s and 1990s. These homes often require updates to their electrical systems to accommodate modern appliances and smart home technology. Our team excels at rewiring, panel upgrades, and installing energy-efficient lighting to enhance your home's functionality.

Plano's hot, humid summers mean many homeowners rely heavily on air conditioning, which can strain older electrical systems. We offer solutions to improve energy efficiency and prevent circuit overloads, ensuring your home stays comfortable year-round.
serviceAreas.localContext: Plano is known for its 1980s-90s subdivisions, where electrical updates are often needed for modern living.

serviceAreas.content:
Legacy Electrical provides top-tier electrical services in Frisco, TX, a rapidly growing city with newer construction homes from the 2000s to 2020s. These modern homes often feature advanced electrical needs, including smart home systems, EV charging stations, and high-efficiency lighting. Our team is skilled in installing and maintaining these cutting-edge technologies.

Despite their newer construction, Frisco homes can still face electrical issues due to the city's hot, humid climate, which demands reliable cooling systems. We ensure your electrical infrastructure supports your comfort and safety, offering everything from inspections to full-system upgrades.
serviceAreas.localContext: Frisco's newer homes often require advanced electrical solutions for smart technology and energy efficiency.
